Previously, bdarnell (Ben Darnell) wrote…
It's not very clear to me what this line is trying to say.
Hmm, the main point not being made clearly is that recovery from node failure will be quicker with more nodes? How would you explain that? I guess the other point I was trying to make - the the processing of queries is distributed just as the data is - isn't really relevant here... --- *Comments from [Reviewable](https://reviewable.io:443/reviews/cockroachdb/docs/1527)*

samples.description: Soufflé is a translator of declarative Datalog programs into the C++ language.  Soufflé is used as a domain-specific language for static program analysis, over large code bases with millions of lines of code.  Soufflé aims at producing high-performance C++ code that can be compiled with the native compiler on the target machine.
samples.name: souffle

samples.description: Update the API to return actual JSON values (no booleans-as-strings and no string integers). This **will** be a breaking change, so this should also include release tracks so future versions can be sufficiently vetted for future consumption.

Update the API to return actual JSON values (no booleans-as-strings and no string integers). This **will** be a breaking change, so this should also include release tracks so future versions can be sufficiently vetted for future consumption.

samples.body:
Allow a pod to be paused (all processes suspended) and resumed (all
processes allowed to continue).

Fixes #267.

Signed-off-by: James O. D. Hunt <james.o.hunt@intel.com>
samples.title: api: Implement PausePod() and ResumePod().

samples.body:
Delete a default template style  (set to ALL or to a specific language)
Look at PHP logs:
`PHP Notice: Use of undefined constant SOME_ERROR_NUMBER - assumed 'SOME_ERROR_NUMBER etc.`

Changed `SOME_ERROR_NUMBER` to 500.

Test again after patch

Simple one that can go in 3.7.3
@rdeutz
samples.title: Fix : Correcting Template style delete Notice

samples.body:
### Features

* support the `prepare` event (#810, @zkochan)

### Bug Fixes

* don't remove prior globally installed packages (#808, @zkochan)
* specless global installation should always install latest (c3f8eaecf123a98c4cea447d309d450c16bb1469, @zkochan)

Version 23.1.1:
-Added feature where it will allow you to delete old files
-Added "_readme" folder to cleanup list
-Updated Translations
samples.name: Version 23.1.1

issue.body:
## Version **6.25.0** of [babel-core](https://www.npmjs.com/package/babel-core) just got published.
Branch Build failing 🚨
Dependency babel-core
Current Version 6.24.1
Type devDependency
This version is **covered** by your **current version range** and after updating it in your project **the build failed**. As babel-core is “only” a devDependency of this project it **might not break production or downstream projects**, but “only” your build or test tools – **preventing new deploys or publishes**. I recommend you give this issue a high priority. I’m sure you can resolve this :muscle:
Status Details - ✅ **bitHound - Code** No failing files. [Details](https://www.bithound.io/github/phenomnomnominal/tractor-plugin-screen-size/2b4e0a1698a4bb83013864019acd4d22e5ccbcd9/files?status=passing) - ❌ **bitHound - Dependencies** 1 failing dependency. [Details](https://www.bithound.io/github/phenomnomnominal/tractor-plugin-screen-size/2b4e0a1698a4bb83013864019acd4d22e5ccbcd9/dependencies/npm#filter-failing-dep) - ✅ **continuous-integration/travis-ci/push** The Travis CI build passed [Details](https://travis-ci.org/phenomnomnominal/tractor-plugin-screen-size/builds/241003356?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
---
Not sure how things should work exactly? There is a collection of [frequently asked questions](https://greenkeeper.io/faq.html) and of course you may always [ask my humans](https://github.com/greenkeeperio/greenkeeper/issues/new).
--- Your [Greenkeeper](https://greenkeeper.io) Bot :palm_tree:
issue.title: An in-range update of babel-core is breaking the build 🚨
